summary: Ukrainian forces struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, a key Russian petroleum transshipment hub in the Baltic region, coinciding with the start of an economic forum in the city. Russia launched widespread drone and missile strikes across Ukraine including on Kharkiv, Dnipro, Kramatorsk, and multiple other cities, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukrainian drones attacked a Moscow-Simferopol bus in Yenakiyevo, killing multiple civilians, while Greece protested a Ukrainian drone found in its territorial waters near Lefkada.
key_points: Ukraine struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, a key Russian petroleum transshipment hub in the Baltic region, on the day an economic forum opened there (Source: @UkraineNow/81638)
Russian forces struck Kharkiv's Kholodnohirskyi and Osnianskyi districts, injuring six people including four with blast injuries and glass wounds (Source: @UkraineNow/81641)
Russian forces struck Dnipro targeting a retail chain warehouse, injuring six people with three hospitalized in serious condition (Source: @UkraineNow/81646)
Ukrainian drones struck a Moscow-Simferopol bus in Yenakiyevo, killing seven people and injuring eleven others (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30630)
Russian forces launched two strikes on Kramatorsk targeting residential buildings, killing three people and injuring four others (Source: @UkraineNow/81650)
Zelensky stated Ukraine is no longer the primary focus of US attention, with Iran being the top priority, and expressed readiness for direct negotiations with Putin (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30655)

Greece sent a protest note to Kyiv after a Ukrainian unmanned surface vessel was detected in Greek territorial waters off Lefkada, calling it a threat to maritime navigation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30652)
Zelensky stated Russia can produce approximately 120 ballistic missiles per month and that Russia also needs Ukraine to be in NATO (Source: @UkraineNow/81654)

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces struck a key Russian oil terminal in St. Petersburg on the opening day of the city's economic forum, marking a significant escalation in strikes on Russian strategic infrastructure. Russia responded with widespread drone and missile attacks across multiple Ukrainian cities including Kharkiv, Dnipro, and Kramatorsk, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukrainian drones also struck a civilian bus in Yenakiyevo, while Greece formally protested a Ukrainian drone found in its territorial waters, and Zelensky acknowledged Ukraine has dropped in US strategic priority behind Iran.

points: Ukrainian forces struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, described as one of Russia's key petroleum transshipment terminals in the Baltic region. The attack coincided with the opening of an economic forum in the city where Putin was scheduled to speak about economic successes (Source: @UkraineNow/81638).
Ukrainian drones struck a Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus in Yenakiyevo, killing seven people and injuring eleven others. All casualties were reported to be civilians (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30630).

Zelensky stated that Russia can produce approximately 120 ballistic missiles per month and claimed that Russia also needs Ukraine to be in NATO. He also stated that Ukraine is no longer the primary focus of US attention, with Iran being the top priority (Source: @UkraineNow/81654).

Zelensky expressed readiness to begin direct negotiations with Putin immediately to end the war, rather than waiting for the US to resolve all global conflicts. He acknowledged that Ukraine is 'in the queue' of US priorities behind Iran (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30658).
In the Nikolaev region, women armed with shovels drove away military commissars, indicating domestic resistance to mobilization efforts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30647).

Greece sent a formal protest note to Kyiv after a Ukrainian unmanned surface vessel was detected in Greek territorial waters off Lefkada, stating it posed a serious threat to maritime navigation and could have resulted in loss of innocent lives (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30652).

The Ukrainian Tax Service uncovered a scheme to illegally siphon at least UAH 198 billion out of Ukraine over 2.5 years through a network of 2,300 risky business entities, with the majority of transactions involving import of goods (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30648).

Russian forces struck Kharkiv's Kholodnohirskyi and Osnianskyi districts, with six people seeking medical assistance. Four victims, including men aged 18, 66, 68 and a 58-year-old woman, suffered blast injuries and glass wounds; two others suffered acute stress reactions (Source: @UkraineNow/81641).
Russian forces struck Dnipro targeting a retail chain warehouse, with six people requiring medical assistance. Five were hospitalized, three in serious condition (Source: @UkraineNow/81646).

Russian forces launched two strikes on Kramatorsk targeting residential buildings, killing three people and injuring four others. A search and rescue operation was ongoing at the scene (Source: @UkraineNow/81650).
Russian forces used prohibited phosphorus munitions to cover Kostyantynivka, according to Ukrainian sources (Source: @UkraineNow/81654).
Russia condemned attacks on St. Petersburg during the economic forum period and attributed them to sponsors of the Kyiv regime, according to Ryabkov (Source: @tass_agency/378908).

DNR authorities and security forces announced enhanced measures to protect roads in the region from Ukrainian drone attacks, stated by Pushilin at the St. Petersburg Economic Forum (Source: @tass_agency/378891).
A Ukrainian drone struck School No. 1 in Melitopol, with bomb disposal experts extracting an explosive warhead from the drone (Source: @tass_agency/378918).

Russian forces launched two strikes on Kramatorsk in Donetsk region, targeting residential buildings and killing three people with four injured. A search and rescue operation was underway (Source: @UkraineNow/81650).
Ukrainian drones struck a Moscow-Simferopol bus in Yenakiyevo, Donetsk region, killing seven and injuring eleven. All casualties were civilians (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30630).
Russian forces used prohibited phosphorus munitions in the Kostyantynivka area (Source: @UkraineNow/81654).
Multiple groups of Russian strike drones were reported moving toward Barvinkove in Kharkiv region along the border with Donetsk region (Source: @eRadarrua/95269).
Russian strike drones were reported in Donetsk region (1 group) and Kharkiv region (7 groups) (Source: @eRadarrua/95271).
KAB missile launches were reported targeting Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/63791).
Russian forces struck Kharkiv's Kholodnohirskyi and Osnianskyi districts, injuring six people (Source: @UkraineNow/81641).
Multiple Russian drone groups were reported across Chernihiv region moving toward Ponornytsia, Sosnytsia, and Borzna from the north, with four drones around Chernihiv city (Source: @eRadarrua/95268).
Russian strike drones were reported in Chernihiv region (11 groups), Sumy region (3 groups), and Kyiv region (1 group) (Source: @eRadarrua/95270).

Drones were reported in the Chernobyl zone (1) moving from Slavutych toward the reservoir, and in Chernihiv region (3) near Men toward Ichnia and Talalaivka toward Ichnia/Parafiivka and Baturyn (Source: @eRadarrua/95272).
A reactive drone was reported in Sumy region heading toward Mykolaivka from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/95226).
Russian strike drones were reported moving toward Ichnia, Korosten, and Snovsk in Chernihiv region, and toward Lebedyn and Sakhny in Sumy region (Source: @eRadarrua/95267).
A group of enemy drones from Sumy region was reported moving toward Poltava region (Source: @kpszsu/63792).
Drones were reported on the border with Belarus in the Pripyat area heading west (Source: @kpszsu/63806).
Russian strike drones were reported in Dnipropetrovsk region (2 groups toward Synelnykove), Zaporizhzhia region (Lezhine, Komyshuvakha), and Kherson region (1 group) (Source: @eRadarrua/95273).
Russian reconnaissance drone (1 unit) and strike drone (1 group) were reported in Dnipropetrovsk region; strike drone (1 group) in Mykolaiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/95257).
Russian strike drones from the Black Sea were reported moving along the border of Mykolaiv and Odesa regions on a northern course toward Tatarbunary (Source: @kpszsu/63799).
Enemy drones were reported in Odesa region west of the regional center near Dachne (Source: @kpszsu/63786).
Russian drones were reported east of Dnipro near Yavornytske (Source: @kpszsu/63778).
Two Shahed drones were reported heading toward Dnipro (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67478).
A strike drone was reported heading toward Dnipro from the east (Source: @kpszsu/63793).
A threat of ballistic weapon use was reported from the east (Source: @kpszsu/63773).
A threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launch was reported from Taganrog, Rostov region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67468).
Information was reported about a ballistic missile launch from Voronezh region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67475).
KAB missile launches were reported targeting Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/63791).
An air raid alert was declared for Kyiv city at 23:26 on June 3, 2026 (Source: @eRadarrua/95279).
Russian drones were reported in Dnipropetrovsk region (2 units) and Zaporizhzhia region (1 unit); strike drone in Kherson region (1 group) (Source: @eRadarrua/95229).
Zelensky stated Russia can produce approximately 120 ballistic missiles per month (Source: @UkraineNow/81654).

key_developments: Ukrainian forces struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, a key Russian petroleum transshipment hub in the Baltic region, coinciding with the opening of the city's economic forum (Source: @UkraineNow/81638).
Ukrainian drones struck a Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus in Yenakiyevo, killing seven people and injuring eleven others, all reported to be civilians (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30630).

Russian forces launched two strikes on Kramatorsk targeting residential buildings, killing three people and injuring four others, with search and rescue operations ongoing (Source: @UkraineNow/81650).
Russian forces struck Kharkiv's Kholodnohirskyi and Osnianskyi districts and Dnipro's retail warehouse, injuring twelve people total across both cities (Source: @UkraineNow/81641, @UkraineNow/81646).

Greece sent a formal protest note to Kyiv after a Ukrainian unmanned surface vessel was detected in Greek territorial waters off Lefkada, threatening maritime navigation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30652).

Zelensky acknowledged Ukraine is no longer the primary US focus with Iran being the top priority, and expressed readiness for direct negotiations with Putin (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30655, @ukr_leaks_eng/30658).

Multiple ballistic missile threats were reported including Iskander-M from Taganrog and launches from Voronezh region, with KAB strikes targeting Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and Donetsk regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67468, @mon1tor_ua/67475, @kpszsu/63791).
sections.threat_assessment: The conflict is experiencing significant escalation with Ukrainian strikes reaching strategic Russian infrastructure in St. Petersburg, while Russia maintains pressure across multiple fronts with drones and ballistic missiles. The threat of ballistic missile use from Taganrog and Voronezh region indicates Russia's continued capacity for long-range strikes. Ukraine faces a complex strategic environment with reduced US attention due to the Iran situation, while also managing a diplomatic incident with Greece over a drone in its territorial waters. The widespread drone activity across northern, eastern, and southern fronts suggests Russia is maintaining multi-axis pressure on Ukrainian air defenses.
sections.regional_response: Greece has formally protested Ukrainian military activity in its territorial waters, signaling potential strain in Ukraine's international relationships and raising concerns about the expansion of military action to the Mediterranean. The US is reportedly prioritizing Iran negotiations over Ukraine, with Zelensky acknowledging Ukraine has dropped in the queue of American strategic priorities. Russia has condemned attacks on St. Petersburg during the economic forum period and attributed them to sponsors of the Kyiv regime. DNR authorities announced enhanced road protection measures against Ukrainian drone attacks.
sections.intelligence_notes: The timing of the St. Petersburg oil terminal strike on the opening day of the economic forum appears deliberately symbolic, targeting Russia's economic narrative. The Ukrainian drone strike on a civilian bus in Yenakiyevo represents a significant incident with potential implications for international perception of Ukrainian operations. The discovery of a Ukrainian drone in Greek territorial waters raises questions about the scope of Ukrainian naval drone operations and potential unintended escalation with NATO members. Zelensky's public acknowledgment of reduced US attention and readiness for direct talks with Putin may indicate a shift in Ukrainian diplomatic strategy. The reported production capacity of 120 Russian ballistic missiles per month provides important context for assessing the sustainability of Russia's missile campaign.
